result.description:
### Figure Description: The Transformer Architecture

This diagram illustrates the full Transformer model architecture, which operates on an encoder-decoder framework. The overall flow is from left to right, depicting the transformation of input sequences into output sequences, adhering to the "Soft Tech & Scientific Pastels" aesthetic for NeurIPS 2025.

**1. Overall Layout:**
The figure will have a crisp, pure white background, providing a clean canvas. It will be horizontally oriented, with the main data flow proceeding from left to right. The entire architecture will be enclosed within a single, large, very light, barely-there pale blue dashed rectangular boundary with gently rounded corners, labeled "The Transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017)" in bold sans-serif text positioned centrally at the top. Inside, it will be clearly divided into two main vertical sections: the "Encoder" on the left and the "Decoder" on the right, separated by a subtle vertical whitespace. Both the Encoder and Decoder sections will be enclosed in their own softly rounded, very light pastel dashed rectangular boxes (the Encoder in a very light mint green, the Decoder in a very light pale lavender), indicating they consist of stacked identical layers. These internal containers will be labeled "Encoder" and "Decoder" respectively, using bold sans-serif text.

**2. Components:**

*   **Input Section (Leftmost):**
    *   A rounded rectangle with a soft sky blue fill and a slightly darker, muted blue border, labeled "Input Embeddings" in bold sans-serif text.
    *   A rounded rectangle with a gentle light lavender fill and a slightly darker, muted lavender border, labeled "Positional Encoding" in bold sans-serif text.
    *   A rounded rectangle with a soft sky blue fill and a slightly darker, muted blue border, labeled "Output Embeddings" in bold sans-serif text, positioned directly below the Input Embeddings, serving as the initial input for the Decoder.

*   **Encoder Section:**
    *   A large vertical softly rounded rectangular container, defined by a very light mint green dashed border, labeled "Encoder (N=6 identical layers)" in bold sans-serif text at its top.
    *   Inside this Encoder container, a single representative "Encoder Layer" will be drawn as a modular block, visually implying stacking. This layer will contain:
        *   **"Multi-Head Self-Attention" module**: A rounded rectangle with a serene medium cerulean blue fill and a slightly darker, muted blue border, labeled "Multi-Head Self-Attention" in bold sans-serif text.
        *   **"Add & Norm" module**: A rounded rectangle with a calming light sage green fill and a slightly darker, muted green border, labeled "Add & Norm" in bold sans-serif text, immediately following the attention module.
        *   **"Position-wise Feed-Forward Network" (FFN) module**: A rounded rectangle with a warm peach fill and a slightly darker, muted peach border, labeled "Position-wise Feed-Forward Network" in bold sans-serif text.
        *   **"Add & Norm" module**: Another rounded rectangle with a calming light sage green fill and a slightly darker, muted green border, labeled "Add & Norm" in bold sans-serif text, immediately following the FFN module.
    *   A clear annotation "x N" or "N times" in sans-serif text will be placed next to the Encoder Layer block to indicate stacking, rather than drawing N layers.

*   **Decoder Section:**
    *   A large vertical softly rounded rectangular container, defined by a very light pale lavender dashed border, labeled "Decoder (N=6 identical layers)" in bold sans-serif text at its top.
    *   Inside this Decoder container, a single representative "Decoder Layer" will be drawn as a modular block, visually implying stacking. This layer will contain:
        *   **"Masked Multi-Head Self-Attention" module**: A rounded rectangle with a serene medium cerulean blue fill and a slightly darker, muted blue border, labeled "Masked Multi-Head Self-Attention" in bold sans-serif text, positioned first.
        *   **"Add & Norm" module**: A rounded rectangle with a calming light sage green fill and a slightly darker, muted green border, labeled "Add & Norm" in bold sans-serif text.
        *   **"Multi-Head Cross-Attention" module**: A rounded rectangle with a deeper, richer twilight blue fill and a slightly darker, muted blue border, labeled "Multi-Head Cross-Attention" in bold sans-serif text, positioned after the first Add & Norm. This module will be visually distinct to emphasize its external input.
        *   **"Add & Norm" module**: Another rounded rectangle with a calming light sage green fill and a slightly darker, muted green border, labeled "Add & Norm" in bold sans-serif text.
        *   **"Position-wise Feed-Forward Network" (FFN) module**: A rounded rectangle with a warm peach fill and a slightly darker, muted peach border, labeled "Position-wise Feed-Forward Network" in bold sans-serif text.
        *   **"Add & Norm" module**: A final rounded rectangle with a calming light sage green fill and a slightly darker, muted green border, labeled "Add & Norm" in bold sans-serif text.
    *   A clear annotation "x N" or "N times" in sans-serif text will be placed next to the Decoder Layer block to indicate stacking.

*   **Output Section (Rightmost):**
    *   A rounded rectangle with a soft, neutral grey fill and a slightly darker, muted grey border, labeled "Linear" in bold sans-serif text, connected to the final Decoder output.
    *   A rounded rectangle with a soft, neutral grey fill and a slightly darker, muted grey border, labeled "Softmax" in bold sans-serif text, connected after the Linear layer.

**3. Connections:**

*   **Input Flow:**
    *   A medium-thickness, solid, muted grey orthogonal arrow from "Input Embeddings" pointing to the "Positional Encoding" box. A gently curved, medium-thickness, solid muted grey arrow originating from the "Input Embeddings" will subtly merge with a medium-thickness, solid muted grey arrow from "Positional Encoding", indicating addition with a small "+" symbol at their intersection. The combined output (a single, medium-thickness, solid muted grey orthogonal arrow) will feed into the first "Encoder Layer".
    *   Similarly, a medium-thickness, solid, muted grey orthogonal arrow from "Output Embeddings" will point to "Positional Encoding" (represented as a shared function or conceptual input), with a gently curved, medium-thickness, solid muted grey arrow indicating addition (with a "+" symbol at the merge point), and feeding into the first "Decoder Layer".
*   **Encoder Layer Flow:**
    *   Straight, medium-thickness, solid muted grey orthogonal arrows will connect the output of each sub-layer to the input of the next within an Encoder Layer, indicating sequential data flow.
    *   **Residual Connections**: Thin, gently curved, solid light grey arrows will elegantly bypass each sub-layer ("Multi-Head Self-Attention" and "FFN"), merging with the output of that sub-layer before entering the subsequent "Add & Norm" module. These arrows signify a "skip connection" for residual learning.
*   **Decoder Layer Flow:**
    *   Similar straight, medium-thickness, solid muted grey orthogonal arrows will connect sub-layers.
    *   **Residual Connections**: Thin, gently curved, solid light grey arrows will bypass each sub-layer ("Masked Multi-Head Self-Attention", "Multi-Head Cross-Attention", and "FFN"), merging with their respective outputs before entering the subsequent "Add & Norm" module.
*   **Encoder-Decoder Connection:**
    *   A prominent, medium-thickness, dashed, muted grey orthogonal arrow will originate from the right side of the "Encoder (N=6 identical layers)" section and point directly to the "Multi-Head Cross-Attention" module within the "Decoder Layer", clearly labeled "Encoder Output" in italicized serif text (for a variable/data stream). This signifies the encoder's output serving as the Keys and Values for the cross-attention.
*   **Output Flow:**
    *   A straight, medium-thickness, solid muted grey orthogonal arrow from the final "Decoder Layer" (specifically, the output of its last "Add & Norm") to the "Linear" layer.
    *   A straight, medium-thickness, solid muted grey orthogonal arrow from "Linear" to "Softmax".
    *   A final medium-thickness, solid muted grey orthogonal arrow from "Softmax" leading out of the entire diagram, labeled "Output Probabilities" in bold sans-serif text.

**4. Groupings:**

*   The entire Transformer model is conceptually grouped by the outermost very light, barely-there pale blue dashed rectangular boundary.
*   The "Encoder" and "Decoder" sections are clearly delineated by their respective softly rounded, pastel dashed rectangular boxes (mint green and pale lavender), emphasizing their modular and stacked nature.
*   Within each Encoder and Decoder Layer, the "Multi-Head Attention", "Add & Norm", and "FFN" components form logical sub-groups, visually maintained by their sequential orthogonal arrangement and consistent styling.

**5. Labels and Annotations:**

*   All component boxes will have clear, centrally aligned text labels in black, bold sans-serif font for readability and modern aesthetic.
*   Arrows representing data flow will have labels where necessary (e.g., "Encoder Output" in italicized serif text, "Output Probabilities" in bold sans-serif text).
*   The "Masked" characteristic of the Decoder's self-attention can be indicated by a small, subtle snowflake icon or a minimal "Mask" text annotation nearby, rendered in a lighter grey.
*   Standard mathematical operators (e.g., a small "+" for addition) will be subtly integrated where connections merge.

**6. Input/Output:**

*   **Input**: "Input Embeddings" (representing embedded tokens of the input sequence).
*   **Output**: "Output Probabilities" (representing the probability distribution over the vocabulary for the next token in the output sequence).

**7. Styling:**

*   **Background:** Pure white, clean and uncluttered, providing maximum contrast and visual breathing room.
*   **Fills (Soft Tech & Scientific Pastels):**
    *   Main structure boxes (Encoder, Decoder, overall Transformer): Very light, desaturated pastels (very light mint green for Encoder, very light pale lavender for Decoder, barely-there pale blue for the overall boundary).
    *   Attention modules (Self-Attention, Cross-Attention): Shades of blue (serene medium cerulean blue for Self-Attention, deeper twilight blue for Cross-Attention) to imply active computation.
    *   Feed-Forward Networks (FFN): Warm peach, suggesting processing and transformation.
    *   Add & Norm modules: Calming light sage green, indicating normalization and aggregation.
    *   Embedding layers (Input, Output): Soft sky blue, representing initial data.
    *   Positional Encoding: Gentle light lavender, distinctively colored to highlight its role.
    *   Linear, Softmax: Soft, neutral grey, for standard output layers.
*   **Borders:** All component box borders will be thin (1pt) solid, slightly darker, muted tones of their respective fill colors, providing definition without harshness. Dashed lines for grouping containers will be slightly thicker (1.5pt) in a corresponding very light pastel color.
*   **Line Weights & Styles:** All primary data flow arrows will be medium thickness (1.5pt) solid, muted grey orthogonal lines. Residual connection arrows will be thinner (1pt), gently curved, solid light grey. The Encoder-Decoder connection will be a distinct medium-thickness (1.5pt) dashed, muted grey orthogonal arrow to highlight its special role.
*   **Arrowheads:** Standard, clean, and proportional arrowheads indicating clear direction of data flow.
*   **Text:** All main text labels for modules will be in a clean, legible, bold sans-serif font (e.g., Roboto or Helvetica Neue) in black. Variable-like labels (e.g., "Encoder Output") will be in italicized serif font (e.g., Times New Roman) in black.
*   **Icons:** A small snowflake icon or "Mask" text annotation in light grey for masked attention; a subtle "+" symbol for addition.
